fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Indiana SLP License Requirements — Fees, Board Contact & How to Apply (2026)
seo.description: Complete guide to Indiana speech-language pathologist licensing. PLA application fees, Praxis requirements, and telepractice rules for 2026.
seo.keywords: Indiana SLP license, speech-language pathologist Indiana, Indiana speech therapy license, PLA

board.name: Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology Board
board.mailing_address: 402 W. Washington St., Room W072, Indianapolis, IN 46204
board.physical_address: 402 W. Washington St., Room W072, Indianapolis, IN 46204

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Indiana.

license_types.title: Temporary SLP Permit
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Earn a Master's Degree in SLP
steps.description: Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination (test code 5331, score 162).

steps.title: Complete Clinical Fellowship
steps.description: Complete 36 weeks of supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours).

steps.title: Submit Application to PLA
steps.description: Submit application with $50 fee through PLA online portal.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

telepractice_text: Indiana permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Indiana issues a Temporary SLP Permit for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Indiana is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Indiana through a compact privilege.

faqs.question: How much does an Indiana SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The application fee is $50 — one of the lowest in the country. Biennial renewal is also $50.

faqs.question: Is Indiana part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: Yes. Indiana has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

faqs.question: Does Indiana allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Indiana permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.
